problem.passage:
11. Read the passage and fill in the blank with ONE word.
【2 points】
In English, the lateral phoneme /l/ has two allophones:
[ɫ], ‘clear l’, [l], and ‘dark l’, a velarized alveolar lateral.
The articulatory difference between the two is that in the
former the back of the tongue is lowered while in the latter
it is raised toward the velum or retracted toward the uvula
(without making contact in either case). Some examples
[ɫ] with [l] and are:
[lɪm], [klaɪm], [lɑk] (1) limb climb lock
[mɪlər], [jɛlou], [bɪlou] (2) miller yellow billow
[mɪɫ], [fɪɫ], [paɪɫ], [mɪɫk] (3) mill fill pile milk
[mɪdɫ], [bʌbɫ], [tʌnɫ] (4) middle bubble tunnel
❚ ❚ ❚
[ɫ] We can see that [l] and are in complementary
distribution. [l] appears in an onset position as in (1) and (2),
[ɫ] while appears in a coda position as in (3). The rule involved
seems to be that velarization takes place whenever /l/ is in
a coda position. However, the cases in (4) cannot be explained
[ɫ] by this rule because is syllabic and constitutes the nucleus,
❚
which is usually occupied by a vowel. By minimally
modifying the above rule, we can obtain a more accurate
rule: /l/ is velarized if and only if it is part of the .
